In addition, thank you so much for your quick response. I believe that once I understand how to add Journey to my laptop, I'll be able to come in and upgrade my review. I do appreciate the hard work your team put in to create this outstanding app. This app does rock! I'm in here every few hours adding pics and info. Thanks!

I absolutely love this app for my daily journaling experience. It has a daily reminder that goes right into my email that I can respond directly from my email. You can attach any type of document along with your log. Many different fonts and colors to choose from. It simply a beautiful app to use. Oh and you can use across my platforms. I synced mine with my Pc with no problem. It has mood notifications you can log each day along with GPS coordinations. This app is very simple and easy to use

Love this Journaling app. I like that it can be locked and use biometrics, that it syncs and has web access and the templates are great too. I'd ideally like to journal by hand but the convenience of always having it with me, privacy/security, templates and the ability to add pics and other metadata make this a winner. I journal much more with this app than by other means. I've also been able to find a middle ground using attached pics to securely save the times when I do write by hand.

I've been writing in a paper journal for 20 years and I thought this would be a more convenient option so I bought the year subscription. On my first entry I had gotten two paragraphs in and the app glitched and deleted everything. For me, this was enough to want a refund and not try again because that's valuable information I don't want to risk losing especially since it happened on the first entry. I tried to contact support for a week with nothing back. I would not recommend.

This app has everything I need–even just at the free level. It has pictures, mood tracking, basic writing, location tracking, and even movement tracking. It really has been the best app I've used so far for journaling. It's especially helpful in keeping track of the little things in a concise and accessible way (pics and moods) so that I can update my bullet journal once a week instead of daily. Thank you Journey!!

I'm glad that I can use this application to keep a record of my life and that I can also back it up to Google Drive and even save it as a ZIP file. I've always tried to be organized and this journal application has been a great help to me. I've been able to go back through my paper journals and actually type in those entries into the application so I have them backed up (I use a wireless keyboard when I use this application). Being able to upload to WordPress is nice, but being able to do it with other applications or websites would be a nice touch as well. Overall, I fully enjoy this application and have recommended it to others.

Perfect. Been using daily for 3 years and plan on using it forever. In fact, I'm writing this review to do my part to help ensure the app sticks around until I retire 30 years from now, lol. Everything you could want between photos, tagging, location, searchability, etc. Managed in the cloud and seamless across platforms. If you want to start journaling THIS is is the app to get.

I came back to Journey after trying a couple of others because they were free - they weren't worth it.... It has more features than I use, but it is nice to know they are there. It doesn't make you follow any rules but gives you lots of personalized opportunities. I love that there are no cutesy icons or characters. Some of the coaching prompts are limiting but they keep you on path. I wish the widget was more inviting. I love it.

The markdown support is absolutely killer. It allows me to write even when I don't have the app handy and know I can put it in. I love having formatted journal entries, and I love that I can have a template I use every day that looks correct and fill it in (I use google keep to store the templates because they don't support that yet). The app works great, it's sync'd on my chromebook, ipad, iphone, and mac at work and my mac at home, so I love having access to all my information and I love that it's all available offline. Even the web view is useful (as that's how I started using it daily). I really love the app, and I'm looking forward to template support.

Love this app. I'm using it to keep a diary related to a biking accident. It's easy to enter notes using speech recognition and I'm adding photos as well.  I am having trouble locating where to find support, though. The app says it syncs content with Google Drive but I cannot find my entries anywhere in Google Drive.   Still, this is a great app for journaling. Highly recommended.

Great app. I got the life subscription on sale so I'd wait for that to come around. Great deal. No complaints here you can attach any files and it all copies to Google drive. There's a separate password for the app which Im sure is nice for some. It also had a quaint memories function where it tells you what you were doing 1 year ago which I like also. The feed is easy to navigate and you can use tags too. Pretty awesome.

I've used this app off and on and it's a fantastic journal app. But being able to use only tags to organize everything drives me nuts. The Journey team needs to add categories along with tags for more organizational options. Add that and this app would be perfection. Until then, I literally can't use it. Please add categories. That would help my mental health when journaling. I'm serious.

A great app for daily writing and journaling. I love using Journey in so many ways: a to do list, a gratatutde list, a daily diary, etc. Journey allows me to collect my thoughts and organize each entry any way I want. From making a video entry, to snapping photos of important events and even making audio recordings...Journey can do it all. Customer service is VERY responsive if needed. After using the Journey app for about a week, I can highly recommend it to anyone serious about journaling.

Used to love using this app. The discounts that's been offered to people on special holidays is only redeemable once and yet you will always get promotional offers for it and can't claim it. In order to even export your journals away from this money grabbing app, you need to be a paying subscriber. Clicked on a "3-day" trial and got charged immediately for the year. That's really bad way to grab people's money.

This app publisher is the greediest I've encountered ever. They don't care about you after they've got your money. I paid for lifetime license and the apps don't work as advertised. There's no feature parity across platforms, and they are artificially slowing down sync via Google Drive so that you are forced to use Journey Cloud sync. When you contact support, they respond very slow and then they ghost you. Do not give them any money. There are better products in market.

I actually enjoy using this app. But there's a couple things that need to be brought up: the price of the premium version of the app and the amount of advertisement for it. I pay $60 at most for a polished videogame that a team of developers have spend tens of thousands of hours creating, and then there's this app with a function barely more advanced than Google keep that wants me to spend $40 each year that I have it? Even if it were a one time purchase that's ridiculously overpriced.

I really enjoy the free version of this app. I think my favorite feature is that it tracks your mood. I also love that it allows you to attach photos and to write seperate entries multiple times a day. My only annoyance is that I cant seem to escape the premium subscription ads every time I open it. oh, and it's also incredibly useful that it allows you to add a password to the app itself so that even of someone has access to your phone, like a spouse, they won't have access to your journal.

Great app! Love the security and personalization options. I like I can attach pictures to a post, perfect for going back to remembering good time t. My only suggestion is on the spot where you put what you are doing it only had a few options (stationary, walking, ....). Most times I'm sitting (riding in the car, on couch, in bed, maybe at a particular location) I wish I could distinguish between them by typing in what I'm doing myself.

I used to love this app. But their in-app upsells have become very aggressive. I finally fell for it because I wanted to try the AI. The AI was not very good, within a few minutes of using it I knew I didn't want the full membership anymore. Customer support said that technically I could have tried it for free and refused to refund because the features are listed on their website, disregarding how misleading the in-app upsells were. So they got my payment I guess but they have lost a loyal user.

This is very simple and convenient to use. Really like the Premium option and being able to journal on my phone and then it ca sync to my Laptop... I'm not able to access all.of the features in Premium. I'm still trying to figure out if it's my settings or the app? Okay up date on trying to figure out this app and the issues that I'm having. So I paid for premium less then 30 days ago for 5.99 then I just got done being prompted to pay 8.99 for premium again today? I also want to create or label different entries and make a almost folder of entries. For example... a folder labeled GOALS etc? How do you do this? I was looking in my settings of the journal app and I was trying to simply access some entries to add more and update them with pictures and then I was prompted to pay 8.99 for premium? What's going on? Do I uninstall this app? I have emailed the help desk that is listed for this app hopefully I will have an update soon.

I used this app years ago when I was going through a lot and needed to get my feelings out. I have a new therapist who encourages me to write. After 2 months my beautiful new journal is practically empty. I just can't seem to get into the practice. Then I remembered this app and downloaded it. I can't believe they kept my entries from 6 years ago! It was incredible reading them. This app is essential for me now. I can write on my phone, computer, anywhere and not worry about finding my journal.

I've used it for a couple of years, it's great, transfered to a new phone easily, hasn't lost one bit of data, easy to navigate and go back and forth. Nothing wrong with that at all. Use it. 1 high bit of Praise I do want to mention, the voice to text on this app is phenomenal, I have never experienced better. It's very easy to hit the microphone and just chat away and the accuracy is so so much better then like my text apps or anything else. I just said this entire compliment without pausing and without looking because the stupid up covers up what I'm typing so I can't see it anyway. However if I get to the end and hit a. I won't change it and you'll know that I did all this Justin morning long paragraph speaking it. Thanks have a great day God bless (Lol, well, it almost made it) (I really am speaking quietly and not clearly and if I had it would have said that I did all this just in one long paragraph, and up = app. There is your proof. LOL. So whatever they use please please sell it to Google for a bazillion dollars and have them use it from now on because if this was my text app or a word doc thete would have been at least seven things to correct up above instead of one

Awesome journal. So many great features. Love that I can put my photos in. It also gives you a "look back" which is great since I've been using it for over 3 years. Also, you can access the your entries from multiple places. And best of all, as I just discovered, when you change/ update your phone, it can just download all your previous entries!! Other features:You can also export them to other places. There are prompts available, a mood tracker and so much more. Love, Love , Love this app.💗💗

This is a great app for daily Journaling. The visualization if the calendar helps you not to break the chain if you are trying to make a habit of of it. The one thing I wish you could do is the ability to make your own journal template. Like if you have certain prompts that you want to answer, you would be able to use a template to create a journal entry.

Very useful and loaded with so many features! It's way more than just a journal. If given permission, it will track where youve been during each entry and map it; it will remind to do an entry and/or to record your mood. The mood is on a 5-scale happy face chart. I have depression and anxiety, so 5 options doesnt cut it and I use a different app for that feature. But add in the photo journal chalenges, the throwbacks, and the tie in to google fit makes the app worthy of the 5 stars I havr given

Great journal. Has some extra costs if you want all options, but the free mode is plenty good for most users. I don't have the money for the premium or lifetime options, but there are a lot of cool add-ons which is great if you use several apps for similar tasks. I used to use the journal dailoo . Basically for the rate your day from with emotes 😢😒🤨🙂🤩. Then journey added the feature and I dropped the other app. Hopefully the trend keeps going and I can get down to 1 or 2 apps.

I enjoy the free version and that I can use it on my phone and my computer. It does give me an excessive amount of popups telling me to subscribe. I realize this is how apps work and how they make money, and I appreciate that this doesn't have ads, but the pop ups can be a little annoying. If I had the funds to buy this I would, but I can't yet. Nice app for journalling on my phone.

Made the mistake if upgrading and now there's a permanent ad in the interface. Incredible amount of water space on the screen. Wish I hadn't bought the desktop version. Unhappy customer will be finding another app to work with. App fails to add multiple pictures when you try to share them - quiet failure ensures that you don't know you wasted your time. Bad interface for choosing location for entries means something like 10 clicks every time you need to make an entry for a location other than the one you're in at the time. Constant upsell and irritation. Clunky interface. Don't waste your time like I did.

I've had the app for about 1 week and so far it's awesome. I was looking for a product that I could use on my MAC and my Android and this so far has worked great on both. Syncing is pretty darn quick once I save my entries. Since this is an e-journal it's great to add updates in the middle of the night without turning on the light and searching for a pen and a regular journal. It's really convenient when you want to document dreams. 👍👍

This is a very good journaling app. It is both convenient and easy to use. I appreciate the convenience of being able to journal anytime that I have access to my phone... which is always. The only improvement to the product that I might want... would be for it to always autosave content when exiting the product. I have lost a few entries here and there.

I've been journaling for over 22 years. I have tried other apps and I found one I love which I won't name. But Journey seems to be superior to them all so I bought the premium version and will use this from now on. I still love to journal in paper journals but I will use Journey as well. Great app. Love it. Beautiful looking and many great features. It just keeps getting better and better. If I could only have one app...this would be the one. I love it that much.

This app literally helped changed my life. I have something like 1500 entries over the last 3 years. I used to struggle badly with depression. One of the most important tools in fighting depression is gratitude, and this app has allowed me to record & track, throughout the years, the moments in my life that are worth tracking. They make it so easy, it becomes second nature. I've downloaded other gratitude journal apps, but this is the one I've stuck with. Thank you, Journey.

PAID ONLY. Free to download, but no free version offered for use, not even a trial. UPDATE: Developer replied, said there is in fact a free version. But I was prompted to upgrade to paid premium, in order to save the first & only entry I attempted. Mind you, I attempted to save this entry multiple times, being prompted to upgrade each time, resulting in the inability to save an entry, therefore making this app FREE ONLY TO DOWNLOAD, on my phone at least. LG Stylo 4, Android 8.1

I have been using this app for years with premium. I only wish there was more formatting options such as bold, underline etc. Also a pinned post! Also would like to see photos IN the entry. Not in a gallery header. I like to write beneath or above my photos commenting them. Other than that, in love with the apps ability to sync across platforms such as phone, tablet and desktop. The app is worth it.

I really like the design and UI of this app. I also really love the organization of having photos, folders, tags, moods for entries. One of the coolest features is the location map to see where you wrote your entries. The biggest cons is that the only sync option is Dropbox. Adding additional sync options like Google drive and iCloud would be better. I also don't like the subscription based model. I would love to just buy the app. Overall excellent app!

After one day of use, I have had a great experience. I appreciate the folders and tags for sorting. The reason I give it 4⭐️ is the sole option of syncing and back up being through Dropbox. One would need to purchase extra storage in Dropbox as time progresses and the amount of journaling increases, especially if photos are included. But for now, I will stick with the app. Its design really is top shelf. I am not one who needs a lot of frills and extras, and this one is perfect. I like it.

I have used this App every day since Aug 2019, and have over 36,000 entries. the app has gotten quite slow now, and the browser extension doesn't sync very well. I love the UI of this app and how I have my various journals organized into folders, but I do wish everything performed a bit better, especially the Syncing with the browser version. I worry one day it just won't be usable anymore, and I can't find anything that can even compete with Diaro.

I looked for a long time....The pro version is roughly $5. well worth it. I was looking for something that had a "to do list" and a "journal" and that let me search a calendar for my entries. I refuse to pay monthly for a journal app (I'd go back to paper first!). This app does everything, plus I can save my work to dropbox, so I'm not held hostage by any developers. (Well done, thanks for the app!!! I will continue to use and suggest your app as long as these things stay available to me)

It's so frustrating! I want to love this app so much. Every year I go looking to see if I can find a journal app I'll truly love. I look at this one every time, and think it's dang near perfect, EXCEPT there's no text formatting at all! No bold, no changing the size, or color, no bullet points for a list, NOTHING! I find it so strange that it doesn't have such a basic function! 😫 Another annoyance is, so far as I can tell, in order to back up and sync you have to use Dropbox. 😠

I'm really liking this app. It makes it super easy to keep up on my journals, which I have never been too good at because I find it rather inconvenient to tote around a notebook and pen/pencil to jot stuff down throughout the day. My one gripe is the limited backup options. I haven't used my Dropbox since 2012, and I don't want to pay for more cloud storage if it gets to that point where my journals take up so much space. I will give this five stars if OneDrive is integrated as an alternative.

I've been trying many journaling and diary apps in search of one that suits my needs. Being able to create different color coded sections with tags, entry dates, weather, and even a map of where I wrote the entry was really cool. It took me a little while to get used to it, but I did and I really like it. I need to keep track of many different things, and Diaro has been working the best for me. I love that Diaro backs up to Dropbox and synchs with all of my devices.

Great useful app! I just wish you could change themes and fonts for every entry. Each time you change a theme or a font, it affects absolutely ALL your other entries. It would be nice to have fonts change for every entry, and themes can be changed like maybe every month or every season. (I bought the subscription, so I don't have any ads.)

I have 3 or 4 diary apps that I use for different purposes and this one is my #2. I enjoy the format and it has been reliable as I switch between devices. There are two reason I won't give it 5* though, one I think it wants way too much info by default. You don't need my location for a diary entry and the other is, needs more/better font options. Other than those, I do recommend.

Good note taking app with quite a few neat features. But, no Google Drive support (only Dropbox). In a 2019 comment they said it was coming soon... I am unsure what "soon" means. Also, no font or formatting controls, that I can find. There is a to do list template, but no way to add checkboxes or bullet points? No font sizes, colors, bold etc. For a writing app, the font and format limits are frustrating, and senseless.

Well, I spent a year exploring and I'm coming back to this one. The interface and interconnectedness of the app are unrivaled. The ability to export and import multiple formats are important. If only I could sync the data with Google Drive instead of being limited to DropBox; at least the data footprint is small enough for the free tier of Dropbox.

I have been using Diaro for many years now. I absolutely love this app. Being able to add my entries on my phone, tablet, or computer is fantastic. I started several years ago putting my grandmother's old journals, and my father's journals into digital format using this App. The search function is wonderful. The only recommendations I have, is it would be nice to be able to go back beyond the year 1900, and I wish that I could put the backup in to Google Drive instead of Dropbox.

This is the very best diary app! I tried several others to make sure I was using the one I'd like the most and I kept coming back to Diaro. (Pro User) I also like the web interface option. I love the ability to share my journal entry w/ my counselor via gmail too. So easy! Oh, and another great thing? The customer service is fantastic! They recently rolled out an update that maybe had a glitch in it, and after a quick email to their support team I was back on track in literally minutes. Amazing!

it could use more features, if it could manage to make them no more obtrusive than the app is already. But for what the app does, it is perfect. You can add pictures to the diary entry, it counts your words and characters, you can set your mood of the day, and you can navigate easily to different entries. It has a fantastic search feature, and you can sort entries in different folders and with different tags. I will use this app for a long time.

Overall I use this app for a lot of note taking on my phone. Meetings, trainings, work notes etc. The Only issue I have with it is that when I am reviewing my notes and need to add to them it does this really annoying and frustrating thing where it automatically scrolls down to the bottom of the note. Which makes editing increasingly frustrating.

I enjoy using Diario. I have a written journal but sometimes want to get my thoughts down when I'm out and about. Having my diary entries backed up on Dropbox is super. I'm happy to pay for premium to not have ads. The app has been working and stable for me for a couple of years and with more than one Android phone.

I liked it a lot but lost data when I changed phones. I had used it since 2015-- on at least 2 previous devices! I didn't realize I [now]had to pay for a subscription in order to get my entries on another phone. I had to wipe my phone when turning it in. If I had known a subscription was needed, I could have at least joined for a month to download my memories. Extremely bummed. I wish I could get my entries back. Might upgrade rating if I can get them back.

I use it several times a day. Started using it in 2012 and I still LOVE it! I'm a writer, so I use it to organize random scenes & ideas, as well as for person ramblings… it's wonderful, especially since I got the pro version & can now save & print from the site. One thing I'd like to see is a multi-select for tagging & not just for folders. :)

This has been a handy little app for several years, but more recent updates have made the ads quite invasive and irritating. For example, when you click to add a photo to your entry, an ad will pop up (sometimes a video ad) that interferes with your selection of photos. And if you back out of the ad, you might end up on your Diaro lock screen and have to re-enter your PIN.

Great, but I'd really like a better way to recover entries. One of my recent entries vanished and I have no way to get it back. It's not in my backup file and that makes me wonder how many other entries were deleted or not backed up. I journal daily and it's really frustrating because I've been having entries sporadically disappearing like this since 1015.

This is a great app for journaling. I've been using it for a couple of weeks now. There is one thing lacking in the app, and that's the ability to use locations other than street addresses. It would be nice to use longitude/latitude to enter a location. I backpack and camp a lot, and if I can't enter locations for my trips, it's only useful to me when I'm home. Aside from that, I love the app.

Diaro is easy to use. When I needed help because I accidentally deleted my ap from my phone the support team got me back on track right away. All my entries were safe and I didn't loose any. I like the organization of this ap. It's easy to add a photo and it is easy to search past entries with a key word. I recommend Diaro as a user friendly way to journal.

As far as a journaling app goes, this is precisely what I've been looking for. I can post from my android device or my windows pc, and the two of them syncronize flawlessly with Dropbox. I also like the export to docx format. clean and simple. The UI is easy to use. The location tagging and weather are both accurate. five stars.

I have found this to be the best diary app for me. It is simple to use, offers flexibility in tagging entries, and is available on all my devices. I like that I can export to a document or PDF which I do at the end of a year. I also like the capability to select entries by folder name or date range. When I contacted support for a usage question I received an email response which answered my question.

The app itself is not bad but I wish there was functionality to save to another location besides Dropbox. I don't use Dropbox & it can be quite expensive if you have enough data stored in there. You can only store 2GB on their free version or it's $9.99/mo. I will up my rating if I could save to Drive. I really don't want to have another app to download to my phone to sync. I would never use Dropbox for anything else. That would be just another app taking unnecessary space on my phone.

this app is making me aware of all of my actions in the short & long term.. For example, I would have never been able to tell you what I had for dinner 2 weeks ago & now I can keep track of all of my eating habits & know exactly what I have been eating & how many calories & anything else I decide to monitor. OK now apply that to any activity you can think of & how you want to track it & that is what this app allows you to do.. its incredible.. plus it's a diary & mood tracker...10/5 rating!

I enjoy Diaro because it gives me somewhere private to write my thoughts. Diaro helps me get creative as writer as well, I use it to type my imagination as a visual so I can get it of my mind. Not to mention it has costomizable settings, you can change the color theme and different fonts, though I hope to see more fonts available without it being premium. More than half of the world population can't afford premium due to the expense, but other than that I recommend Diaro.😁

Excellent journaling, notetaking, habit tracking, memory keeping, mood tracking, research organizing, lesson planning, record keeping, list making, recipe collecting, LIFE ORGANIZING app! It has all the essential elements I was searching for in one single app that I was using several apps in a combined and complicated effort to accomplish my goals and organize my life. Thank you for an excellent product that simple, useful and user friendly!

Easy to use, great app, I may have to buy the pro version so I can back up to the cloud but other than that I am very pleased. I broke down and spent the 6.99 for the pro version. I love it. It backs itself up and unlocked some sweet tweaks!! I unlocked the pro version. It's awesome. I have really learned alot about myself from journaling everyday

I have used countless notes apps, Journal apps and find Diaro to be the best app to date. It's full of features and ways to save your works and you export to Google Keep, save all your entries into one large PDF file with complete control on how it looks. Just about a perfect application. My only negative is Dropbox is currently the only way you can backup your Journal which for someone like me I tend to use the Google ecosystem. Other than that I'm very pleased with the app.

I think my favorite feature is the ability to duplicate entries. I use this app to track my workouts and it's so easy if I repeat a workout to just make a duplicate and just change the date and minor details, without having to manually copy and paste yourself. Sizing photos is a challenge. The cropping and resizing feature is really weird, and it took some trial and error to figure out the optimal aspect ratio to get my pics to fit.

I love love love love the reminder to journal, the attachment capability, the DropBox back-up, and the folders and tags for organization? I had low expectations after trying and failing gamification apps for goal completion, and it turns out this was all I needed. My only suggestion is being able to enhance the text - fonts, bold, italic, strike through, underline. The app is not at all atheistically pleasing. But I'll be here for the function regardless, waiting for the cute updates.

UPDATE: Thank you so much for correcting this issue so fast! This is my favorite diary app by far! -------------------------------------- I have had this app for years and have never had a problem with it, but all of a sudden today it started randomly crashing on me. So I updated it and now it won't even open. Super frustrating. Please fix this asap!

Edit: 1/19/22 I've had this app since 2014 and all has been well. I'm a pro member. However, lately - I'm noticing when I'm typing, everything will randomly delete at times and I'll have to hit the undo button multiple times. As well as mid typing the screen will go white and exit out of the entry. Not sure if this has to do with the app itself or the recent update on my S21+. Overall, still the best diary app as I've been using for the last eight years.

Loved it. I found the paid version very reasonable since I don't like subscriptions. Also, the synchronization with dropbox is a plus for me, rather than using the app's cloud. The other customized options are good too like the folders and tags. Finally, I like the flixibility of adding an entry on any day, and not necessarily on that exact day. The reason I didnt give five starts is the moods part; having only standard 5 moods isn't practical and I wish you add more or leave it to the user.

Great app! The only thing I would change is for the 'forgot security code' to be optional and or have some questions or a hint (for your password,) rather than sending you an email with the information blandly available. The logic behind this is unless you don't have email on your phone, whoever is trying to get in, (it could be anybody going after your diary,) is going to receive the email with the password in side. I think this is really important to add as a security feature.

I really do like this app. I've made 150 entries across many years. The ability to tag, sort in folders, and organize entries in other ways is really powerful. I paid for Pro and I sync between my PC, tablet, and phone. I really wish they'd add formatting to entries! Even just Bold, Underline, Bullets, and Numbered Lists would be awesome.

Updating my review because of the very quick and helpful customer support. Lost all of my data in a faulty automatic sync but the team was able to help me recover years worth of memories. Diaro is one of the first great diary apps with several great features, and a dirt cheap price for a lifetime membership to the paid tier. I'm knocking off a star because the web interface and syncing are unresponsive and clunky for my taste since I tend to write a large amount of entries. Still worth the $.

This app is the best and has solved my ongoing struggle to keep a journal with my busy lifestyle. Right now, I am waiting outside my daughter's work, and I'm using the time to journal. I can pull my entries up on the computer, as well, and print them out, so I can turn this into a more long-lasting journal. Everything is user-friendly. Text is easy to enter with the keyboard or with voice, and pictures are easy to attach. Thank you for this wonderful app!

I have been using diaro for several years and it's one of my go to favorite apps for all kinds of stuff like taking notes, journaling sorting notes etc it's like having some of these sorting capability of an Excel sheet displayed in an elegant fashion and easy to use. not Kiki at all. I bought the pro, worth every penny. it also became a great tool for me to lose 60 lb. I just ripped out my phone took a picture of everything I ate so I had an accurate food to work with.

I have tried several e-journals and found them to be cumbersome at best, definitely not easier than pen and paper. But, this one I actually like, it's easy to sync when needed, I can edit my entries without an IT degree and I love the fonts. I didn't think I was a difficult person until trying to find an e-journal, this one is a winner.

I've always wanted to be a person who journals regularly. I romanticized the idea of paper and pen journals, but once I downloaded Diario, I've actually stuck with it! Best features: 1. When you add photos, a prompt ask if you'd like to update the entry based on the time stamp (great if you get behind) 2. You can add entries from ANY date, I collected all my old, only a few entry, journals and put the contents in the app so I have it in one place. Love love love this app

I picked up this journal option for two reasons. It syncs, and I can download entries for printing. Without a doubt the best journal app I have found. Update 11/30 Still very pleased with this app overall. The different folders allow for individual journals, ie. work, personal, etc. Still would love to see Google Drive and Microsoft One Note as storage options. Also would like to be able to put in family entries before 1900.

This has the makings of a great app, the interface is clean and easy to use and the price cant be beat. Beware though - there is no ability to underline, bold, use italics, fonts, bullets, etc... there is zero text customization options. This makes creating legible journal entries incredibly difficult. They have it on the roadmap apparently, but its been "in planning" for 8 years now. I could learn how to code from scratch and create that option faster than they can improve their own software

I've had this app for several months already. I'm not a heavy user (trying to do photo journaling) and I haven't used it on a computer yet, but so far I like the app. Convenient, easy to use and allows me to add pictures. I like the general format of the layout and the ability to customize the color scheme. Edit: Almost 4 years later and still using the pro version of this app.

I've been using Diaro since 2014. After a long battle between manual or digital journaling, I've decided that Diaro is the best journaling process for me. I have a tendency to jot down my random thoughts and want the accessibility and portability when it happens throughout the day. Diaro has photos, locations, folders, and tags. Love the editable templates. The best part is On This Day allows me to reflect on the past 10 years. What was I doing and where I was each year.

I wanted a more robust journaling app than the one I'd been using. Something that I could use on my phone on the go, or at my computer when at home. I went with Diaro and the premium service to get rid of the minimal ads there were. The developer was super responsive in helping me transfer the data from my old app to this one, an app that was not in the supported transfer list. That is the sort of customer experience that keeps customers for life. Thank you!

I have been using Diaro for 3 years. I absolutely love it. I use different background colors for my categories to make things easy to find, and the search bar also helps me find entries all the time. This is my go-to app for journaling, random notes, random lists, recipes, ideas, favorite quotes, random thoughts, etc. I can access my entries easily from the computer as well, because it syncs to an account through your email. I just wish it had basic font formatting (bold, italics, underline)!!!

I've been using Diaro for years, and have been very pleased with it. I have used it both as a diary, and also as a medical logbook. My favorite feature is the cloud backup, which had saved all my entries when I lost the data on my phone. Even the free version allows you to backup to Dropbox, which is great. I had to contact support once, and they were very efficient and helpful. An excellent app overall.

I love this app! I've used other note taking apps and word processers but nothing really compared to physical journalling until I found this one. It has a simple interface and it's accessible through all your devices, so no excuses to not write. It also makes note of the weather, location (if you want), and your mood which I find really interesting and helpful for when looking back at older entries. It also offers the ability to add tags and folders so you can search for specific entries!

Use it as a boat log and is easy and simple to use. One of the best apps I have used. Everything about it just works! If I had any complaints it would be with the location feature. Great feature but it doesn't seem to update unless you are near a place it recognizes Since I use it as a boat log rarely am I near a place it reconizes. It would be nice if it just did lat/log if it didn't recognize a location. You can add it manually but I'm to lazy to do it. Very minor complaint!

I love this app because it's so much like an written diary but better. It works flawlessly, never had any problems. I can record all my thoughts by dates and recall them easily. I love that I can add the categories and the title preview given. I love that there is both the desktop and android version that keep in sync. I've owned this for years and just came back to it and all my notes were still there. I just purchased the premium version. I use it everyday.

I must've tried every journaling app available on Android and I can say with confidence that Diaro is one of the best there is. I like the aesthetics and the feel of the app, but also all the different features it offers - such as tagging, separate folders for different types of journals, customizable templates... The annual subscription was also very reasonably priced. Overall, a happy camper!

This is a fantastic app for journaling and well worth the price of the Pro version. It has pretty much every feature you could want in a journaling app. Love the Dropbox sync, tags, journaling stats, and "On This Day" features. Only improvement that's needed is in security. All of the journal entries are stored plaintext in an XML file that can be viewed outside the app by anyone who has your phone. Encrypting the data at rest would be a massive improvement. Hope to see this in a future update.

The only thing I find concerning is that I found a folder that says "no folder" and it contained a large amount of entries that I had placed in previous folders. I'm happy those entries didn't just disappear completely but it appears that every few entries your entry doesn't get saved inside the folder you wrote it in and it gets kicked down to "no folder". If it's doing this with only a couple hundred entries I'm afraid of how messed up it will get when I have thousands of entries.

Having tried different diary apps, I really like Diaro so I upgraded to Pro. It has many features I need. It can sync across devices and the price is good. However, using w/ Win 10 & Chrome broswer from the web, when I click on an entry, there are always those light solid ruler lines that show up on the page, regardless of the design I picked. When I use Diaro from my smartphone, I don't see those lines. Since I do a lot of writing from the web, can the lines can be turned off in next upgrade?

I bought the Pro version which makes my life easier connecting all my devices and laptops entries so that I can write from anywhere. Which is something I miss while trying other apps. Also this app is flexible and very easy to use. The only thing that needs some modification is that it should be more styles and themes to choose from and to have nighr vision theme automatic time activation feature.

Stumbled upon this app by happy accident. I needed something to track my diet daily and be able to print out for my doctor. Well, this app does that! Lays out the day like a calendar, select the pre-made template, timestamp if wanted and away we go! Couldn't be easier. And fully printable! But there lies the problem. When I printed it from my phone, it didn't scale to the paper properly and cut off the edges. 26 pages of useless papers and wasted ink. Next time I'll save to PDF first.

I have been using the paid app since 2015 with great success, but in the last few months, it has become slower and tags don't seem to work correctly on the web version. I have sent requests to support a couple time regarding this matter and I have received no response at all. Very disappointing. I will not be renewing my subscription. Previous review from 2015 - This thing does everything I want it to do, plus the online features of the paid version makes it even better, and easier.

I am a Diaro Pro member. Why hasn't emphasis formatting been made available yet? It would be extremely helpful and beneficial, very useful, would make a big change in the finished product for users. Bold, italics, underlining, highlighting, color emphasis, etc. - I'm sure it would be appreciated by a large majority of the users.

The sync feature works beautifully and secures my entries. I highly recommend Diaro. I even type entries on my Windows desktop. Help! I had to power wash my Chromebook and as of September, 2017, I cannot find Diaro for Chromebooks. Is someone going to fix this? I like Diaro the best and I want it back. Update: 11/21/2020 Some time ago, I was able to find Diaro again for my Chromebook. I am happy. I also use it to compose entries for other writing projects. I'm tickled to have it back!

Only started using today, but I have downloaded eight diary apps looking for a full feature set and only this app checked the boxes of features I have been looking for at an economical cost. With Diario, I have multiple diaries, to-do lists, templates for entries, and I can export entries with pictures from a date range to .docx for external use. So, Diario is seriously useful.

I would like to pay only to update when *I* want, IF I need the update, not continually in advance for promises, or for updates I may not need. Others offer this. -> Also ridiculous are personal attacks for objective observations. And I never asked for "free." -> What's "ridiculous" is renting software. A "fair price" might include a reasonable option to buy outright, not have to pay $8 to $36 annually simply to remove ads, export, and sync, which other developers include in their one-off price.

Security Features SUCK! It's basically secure enough to be useless! I do have Pro version which means I paid for it! Save ur money! Had I known screen shots are prohibited I would NOT have purchased it. I like to make lists & put in my gallery for easy access but you cannot do a screen shot!. Huge Limiting factor as far as I'm concerned. When I tried to contact or send this to support it kept looping me. I actually don't think there is any support for this app anymore. Piss Poor service!

I've been using this app since I was 14 and I'm now 22! This is my favorite diary app❤️I've actually recommended it to a lot of my friends :D I got the pro membership about two years ago and I love being able to upload my entries into Dropbox :D I also love the folders option! I've written over 500 dreams and I can just click the dreams folder to read them and it's so convenient! I'd love to see audio recordings and the option to have one entry be in multiple folders in an update one day!:D❤️

Update to my Update: yes, Dropbox has a free option but it's annoying as hell. I hate Dropbox. Update: i found a couple of very annoying issues. They are not deal breakers and i sent a note to Support. Let's see if they respond. (Nope, not a word) Original: I've been using this for a week now and so far, I have only one issue (saw this from the start). The ONLY backup option is Dropbox which I dropped a very long time ago. The app itself has not given me any troubles.

Putting 2 stars so I can get a reply from the creator. I accidentally pasted something long in the search bar, which completely destroyed the app and wouldn't open no matter what I did. I uninstalled and reinstalled. I'm Pro, so my entries were backed up, thank the Gods. I have nearly 3000 entries because I use this as a research book. However, the images in the entries never came back. It says 2/6, so it knows they're there, but they don't show. I'm absolutely devastated. I lost so many pics.

I used do use another app the name if which was one character longer than Diario, and it shared most if the same letters, but it had limited options. I always wondered why they didn't allow attachments, specifically photos, but the developers just said that the ability to load photos isn't what the intended for the app to do, disk they were bit planning to add that feature even down the road, so I was an easy poach to this awesome app. I'd say A LOT more if I weren't shackled to < 500 spaces! :/

I really liked this app until I switched phones. I figured that since we had a login it would keep our data even if we didn't upload to Dropbox. I was wrong and I went back to my old phone that wasn't working and it had complete factory reset and I lost everything. I'm definitely looking for a better app that can fight against this. I lost ALLL my high school journal entries. 5 years later that's something I would love to look back on. Thought it was save. Anyone found a way to get them back?

I have been using this app for about 5 years now. You have the option to place a password on it which is free of cost. It is also AD FREE!!! You can categorize your writings and view them by that specific category as well - which helps. There is also an option to write online! After awhile, I like to PDF my writings, which i'm not sure if that option is for PRO only, but its worth having.

I absolutely love Diaro, been using it for a few years now. I use it for everything password keeper, lists for whatever store i need to go to, and i write in it like a you would any diary or journal, I havent had not one issue with the app whatsoever, and everything I have ever wrote in it is there no timing out no glitches no error prompts so contact customer service or the app developer. Everything is as it should be. Thank you for making an awesome easy to use app.

Great Diary (journal) app. The best one around nowadays. ** update, March 2026. I've used this journal app for more than 5 years. I love the layout on a tablet. You can see your notes, calendar, and folders all at once in landscape. That's so convenient. I have other note apps but always get drawn back to Diaro.

Just starting to journal at 41. This app makes it easy. I asked ChatGPT to create a bunch of writing prompts and loaded them as templates, so I always have something to write about. The location, tagging, pictures, and mood features make it easy to find past journals to reflect. At least for now, I find it extremely motivating. I wish I had this 20 years ago, when I failed at starting journals multiple times.

I'm very happy with my choice of Diaro for a diary. It's easy to use, I can track my moods, its customizable, different folder and tags, location tags. I just love it. The best I've used for this purpose, plus you can lock with your fingerprint and keep entries for your eyes only!!

Handy and beautiful app. I love how it allows you to add a daily photo roll and keep more than one type of journal, plus searchable tags. (I keep separate journals for my garden upgrades and aquarium tracking.) Would give it 5* and actually pay for the Pro version if you could backup data and sync devices via Google Cloud. (Maybe I can and just haven't discovered that yet?)

Great customer service! for whatever reason (I did something dumb 🤦‍♀️) I suddenly lost a very special journal entry...just disappeared into.... la la land? I was freaking out (because of how long I had spent writing it). emailed asking for help. customer service was able to retrieve what I lost in under an hour. *hallelujah* can't wait to print all my journal entries into a book soon!

I've had this app installed on my tablet for several years. I missed a recent upgrade, but since the app was working fine I let it go. Until the entire tablet crashed. After my tech guy had helped me to restore it, I decided to upgrade to premium so I could back up my files. I'm no techie, so after an hour of trying to figure out why it wasn't backing up, I contacted the developer by email. Not only did they reply within 24 hours, they told me exactly what I needed to do to back up the files. (It wasn't brain surgery, but I never would have guessed it.) That kind of personal attention--real help from a real person who's never met me--makes all the difference when choosing which app to use. And did I mention I love the app? I guess you figured that out.

[Update:] I appreciate the quick response from the developer. I misunderstood...The subscription option is necessary for future development, but my Pro status is not affected. While I am not a fan of ongoing fees for any app, I don't mind dealing with ads, donating, or paying a one-time premium to keep apps perpetually free. It's a wonderful app that I can't be without! [Original Review:] Starting to charge subscription :( I'm a Pro user. There should always be a free option.

This is the best journal app ever! I install it on every Android and iOS device I own and I own more than 5 in total. I have tried at least 10 other journal apps and this is the best one by far. It stays synced on all my devices automatically via dropbox. I can also do manual backups to a storage device. It allows organization and tags so that entries are categorized and easily found when necessary.

I replaced another appwith Diaro and I'm very happy with the result. Diaro is clean and fast with a very intuitive UI. I like how it lets me sync to Dropbox and does not try to insist I store my data on Google drive like Journey ( the main reason I switched). Diaro is easy to use on mobile and to add pictures to entries and the website  (with Pro version) is excellent and the syncing is fast. I liked Journey but I really like Diaro. I exported all my data  and imported all into Diaro. Diaro works smoothly and just like I want it to. Deserves all 5 stars!

Love, love, love. Changed my life in terms of recalling the weeks events. I have a job where I run around like a chicken with a head cut off and this keeps everything in line. I use tags as different projects that I'm working on for different clients, that way I can use multiple tags on a single entry, and I use folders for separating personal and business. This is so cool, love that it records weather and location. The only thing I could suggest is integrations with to-do apps!!!

I've been using Diary since 2015. I've seen many changes since then and most of them positive. The creator cares about the app and it shows. I hope to continue using it for years to come. It is my go-to for all my writings, allowing me to write at the drop of a hat and updating seamlessly for rapid access across platforms.

This is so amazing! It's a lot better than most apps I've used. There's so much stuff, this app kinda saved my life in a way! I'm so grateful that it exists! Please keep making improvements to it! 😍 Edit: Um, this has happened before but after a while, but all of the thumbnails and pictures won't show up anymore. I'll click on the note and even then, the photos are still blank. The app says they're all there, but again, the pictures/photos don't show up. Does anyone else have this problem??

After trying many journal apps, Diaro was the best combination of a clean presentation of entries, ease of use, excellent searching (e.g., use of quotation marks for exact searches), synch across multiple devices, great photo and camera integration, reliable backup, and several other excellent features. An essential tool for my everyday life, Journaling life events & my solutions for technical issues I've encountered. Glad to pay a subscription, to ensure continued updates / improvements

I'm actually pretty well satisfied with this diary app. It's got pretty much all of the features one could find useful and then a few small extra touches I didn't know I'd like to have... I'd only make a few small tweaks here and there, but TBH it's not really worth bringing them up. So if you're interested in a digital daily diary, I would like to recommend this one. You'll probably like it.

I like it, but would like more choices in fonts and formatting as well as to see separate notebooks. When I changed phones, ino longer had pictures loading. For both old and new entries. Still unable to get pictures back. I cannot type in the path to the folder. It's hard set to certain locations.

I've been dealing with a lot of issues in my life lately, and this app is really very helpful to me. I have a lot of journals, and I use them, but if I'm out at an appointment or somewhere, where's it's hard to whip out a book, this app is truly a life saver. No matter where or what I'm doing, this app is available 24/7.. No matter if you want to write about your day, an issue your having or just to write. This app is EASILY accessible and free.. I HIGHLY recommend this app.

I got this app through Play Pass and I'm really glad I have tried it! It is absolutely fantastic for keeping notes and tabs on my life without writing page upon page of rant like I tend to do with a paper diary, lol. This encourages me just to record the juicy bits, which is perfect for my needs as a writer... it encourages me to spend the majority of my energy on the actual writing instead of getting hung up on the recording.

Incredible and thoughtful. If you use it correctly you'll find that you can organize things very well. I do have a slight thing you guys can improve on, it's on how to use the app. I had to use it for quite a while to understand what goes where. Maybe a tutorial? But overall I think this is perfect for those looking for a private, simple, and unique diary.

Lost several months of entries. Needed to factory reset my phone, so I made sure to run a manual backup of the Diaro data. Gives you the choice of "SD" or "dropbox". I selected SD card, and it showed as having backed under SD tab within app. Ran the reset, went to recover no backup file existed. Turns out that even though the app says SD on the tab, actually uses internal storage unless you MANUALLY change the directory to SD card. Very misleading, lost months of data, incredibly disappointing.

Really like Diaro. Does exactly what I hoped and is a pleasure to use. I like that the data lives on DropBox. Would suggest a couple enhancements... Selecting multiple pictures at a time to attach (Done), automatic backup to Dropbox on a schedule, and a quick way to enter an entry via voice. Overall, love this app.

I love this app a lot and I use it for my classes so that I can note down and study on the go even when I'm home on my computer. This so has become an important tool that I used daily for this. My only request is that there be a text editor to be utilized such as bold, italics, underline, times New Roman fonts, etc. This would seriously make this app a life long tool to stick with if these are added to it! I absolutely appreciate the folks who are behind making this app and keeping it alive. Buying this premium version of the app is well worth it.

Love this app. Had it a few years now (since 2016). I've only lost entries (as some have complained about) by forgetting to sync to drop box. Seems really stable other than that. I use it between my laptop and mobile and haven't had any problems. Love that I can use tags and folders to organize.

I'm really enjoying the app. My only complaint is the paid version is a subscription. I'd upgrade if it were a one time thing. Aside from that there are lots of nice features like folders and tags for organizing entries. Customizable colors and backgrounds. I also like that I can add pictures to each entry.

I like the lock, the font changes/style changes you can make to the app. I like that you can add longer titles than most apps. I like that you can tag and folder and emote on each entry. I don't even mind the ads- but I wish that after long ads it wouldn't ask me to sign in AGAIN 😒. That's a bit annoying.

I have been using this app daily for a few years and it has been ideal for my purposes. I upgraded to the Pro version early on. I mostly access this from the app, but it is useful at times being able to access the data through the web interface. I have multiple classifications of things I capture each day based on things I want to monitor in my life. It handles this very well and keeps them separated in the interface nice and cleanly giving me the option to customize what I need. Well worth it. Thanks!!

I use it as a Log Book, alongsude my pen and paper notebook/logbook. This product is better than Microsoft One Note or Google. It synchronizes across all my devices(iPhone, Android Tablet, Windows 8) seamlessly and effortless. I seldom(twice in 10years) purchase the pay $xy.zz per month option. However I am so happy with thi App that the less than $8.00 for a lifetime was as beautiful offer as the performance. I can insert photos, documents so far.

A little disappointed. For this lacks some of the key features I like using such as: Bold, Italics, different colored text & font sizes, etc... and not having the tools needed readily available on a toolbar. I have loved the Diaro app since 2010 and was so excited to have run a crossed it.

This is a great app. The only thing that would make it better for me would be a "time stamp" option for each time I write something in the same "entry". (I like to have one "entry"/file per day, with various items posted throughout the day.) ☆☆☆update. .. 10/2020. They added a time stamp! As if this app wasnt awesome anyway. And, the search feature is so handy! Keep up the good work.

serpPaaQuestions: Quick verdict: Top daily planner apps at a glance

Site diary app app for android
Site diary app app for iphone
